Explosive Media, an Iranian 🇮🇷 government-linked group producing Lego-style AI videos, just published this disturbing video, insinuating the Iranian regime is behind the death of US 🇺🇸 Senator Lindsey Graham and threatening others, including @LauraLoomer. The video features the letters "KT," which may stand for "Kill Trump." "Monica" in the video likely refers to Monica Witt, a former US 🇺🇸 service member and counterintelligence agent who defected to Iran in 2013 and was indicted in 2019 on charges of spying for Iran.

RT by @NotWoofers: Russians showed their «Orion» with S8000 LM. The loitering munition «Banderol» is manufactured at the «Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Production Plant» by JSC «Kronshtadt». The «Banderol» LM is equipped with a warhead weighing up to 150 kg and is capable of covering distances of up to 500 km at a speed of 500 km/h. As a warhead, it may employ the OFAB-100-120 fragmentation-high-explosive aerial bomb (total weight — 123 kg, explosive charge mass — 46 kg). The primary carrier platform for the LM is the «Orion» UAV. Adaptation of the munition for deployment from Mi-28N attack helicopters is currently underway. The propulsion system consists of a Chinese miniature turbojet engine «Swiwin SW800pro», which is commercially available for purchase on the global online marketplace AliExpress. The «Swiwin SW800pro» turbojet engine is manufactured by the Chinese company «Swiwin» for use in aeromodelling applications. It operates on kerosene or diesel fuel. Engine specifications: thrust — 800 N (80 kgf); diameter — 20.8 cm; length — 45.5 cm; weight — 8.5 kg. The primary direction of further development for the LM has been identified as improving strike accuracy through modifications to the munition's electrical circuit, specifically the installation of jam-resistant antenna modules «Kometa-M12». One of the key vulnerabilities of the munition, identified in late 2025, is the deformation of the LM's wings during their deployment at the initial stage of flight. As a solution to this issue, a decision was made to introduce an additional factory-level inspection to verify the absence of wing deformation upon deployment — specifically, a supplementary wing-opening check conducted directly on the production floor. This defect had not previously been observed. The most probable cause of the wing deformation has been attributed to either the supply of metal of an incorrect grade for wing production, or a breach of manufacturing technology during the production of the said metal.

Did the Patriot system fail or did the US 🇺🇸 allow those sheds to be hit? There was speculation in October 2024 that Israel 🇮🇱 allowed some Iranian 🇮🇷 missiles to hit air base runways or empty hangars because the cost of repairing the damage was less than the cost of an Arrow interceptor. Arrow interceptors cost $2-$3.5 million a piece. A single Patriot missile costs ~$4 million.
